categories.title: Best Practices
categories.description: Essential driving rules and safety tips
categories.introLead: Swipe through each scenario. Good habits reduce risk for you, your passengers, and everyone on the road.

introSlides.title: Seatbelt
introSlides.body: Fasten your seatbelt before moving off and make sure every passenger is belted too. A seatbelt keeps you in place if you brake hard or are involved in a collision.

introSlides.title: Mirrors
introSlides.body: Adjust your mirrors before you start. Check them — and your blind spots — before changing speed, direction, or lane.

introSlides.title: Following distance
introSlides.body: Keep a safe gap behind the vehicle in front so you can stop in time if it brakes suddenly. Increase the gap in rain or at higher speeds.

introSlides.title: Stay alert
introSlides.body: Avoid phone use and other distractions. Scan ahead for pedestrians, cyclists, and hazards — especially near crossings and junctions.

modules.title: Intro: safe driving habits
modules.description: Short reference on everyday safe driving practices.

modules.title: MCQ: best practices
modules.description: Six questions on habits every driver should follow.

categories.title: Traffic Rules
categories.description: Sri Lankan traffic regulations and laws
categories.introLead: Swipe through each topic. Knowing the law helps you drive legally and avoid fines.

introSlides.title: Driving licence
introSlides.body: You must hold a valid licence for the class of vehicle you drive and carry it when driving.

introSlides.title: Vehicle documents
introSlides.body: Keep insurance, revenue licence, and other required papers up to date and available if asked.

introSlides.title: Speed limits
introSlides.body: Obey posted limits and drive at a speed safe for conditions — even when below the limit.

introSlides.title: Right of way
introSlides.body: Give way where signs, markings, or rules require it. Never assume other drivers will stop.

modules.title: Intro: traffic rules
modules.description: Licence, documents, and basic legal duties on the road.

modules.title: MCQ: traffic rules
modules.description: Six questions on regulations and required documents.

categories.title: Parking
categories.introLead: Swipe through each scenario. Park only where allowed and check signs before you stop.

introSlides.title: Where not to park
introSlides.body: Do not park on crossings, junctions, bus stops, or where signs or lines prohibit stopping.

introSlides.title: Parallel and angle parking
introSlides.body: Park within marked bays, close to the kerb, and leave room for other vehicles to pass.

introSlides.title: Hill parking
introSlides.body: Use the handbrake and, where needed, turn wheels toward the kerb so the car cannot roll.

introSlides.title: Clearance
introSlides.body: Leave enough space at driveways, fire hydrants, and pedestrian routes.

modules.title: Intro: parking rules
modules.description: Where you may park and common restrictions.

categories.title: Vehicle Control
categories.introLead: Swipe through each skill. Smooth steering, braking, and gear use keep you in control.

introSlides.title: Steering
introSlides.body: Hold the wheel securely and steer smoothly. Look where you want to go, not just at the bonnet.

introSlides.title: Braking
introSlides.body: Brake early and progressively. Sudden hard braking can surprise traffic behind you.

introSlides.title: Gears
introSlides.body: Select the right gear for your speed and terrain. Use lower gears on hills or when you need more engine braking.

introSlides.title: Mirrors before manoeuvres
introSlides.body: Signal and check mirrors and blind spots before turning, changing lane, or pulling away.

modules.title: Intro: vehicle control
modules.description: Steering, pedals, and gears — the basics.

modules.title: MCQ: vehicle control
modules.description: Six questions on operating the vehicle safely.

categories.title: Safety Procedures
categories.introLead: Swipe through each procedure. Stay calm, get to a safe place, and warn other road users.

introSlides.title: Hazard lights
introSlides.body: Switch on hazard warning lights when stopped in a dangerous place or when your vehicle is a hazard.

introSlides.title: Warning triangle
introSlides.body: Place a warning triangle behind your vehicle on busy roads when it is safe to do so.

introSlides.title: Breakdown
introSlides.body: Move off the carriageway if possible. Stay out of live traffic while waiting for assistance.

introSlides.title: Accidents
introSlides.body: Stop if required. Check for injuries, call emergency services if needed, and exchange details.

modules.title: Intro: safety procedures
modules.description: What to do in breakdowns and emergencies.
